item.title: Chess Records
item.description: Founded: 1950 (as Chess Records; predecessor Aristocrat Records from 1947) Location: Chicago, Illinois (2120 South Michigan Avenue, 1957–1967) Founders: Leonard Chess, Phil Chess Key years: 1950–1969 Subsidiaries: Checker Records (1952), Argo Records (1955; renamed Cadet, 1965) RYM The sound of 2120...

item.title: Philles Records
item.description: Founded: 1961 Location: New York City / Los Angeles Founders: Phil Spector and Lester Sill Key years: 1962–1966 Key genres: Girl group, Pop soul RYM The drums on “Be My Baby” hit like a door swinging open into a room with no back wall.

item.title: Sun Records
item.description: Founded: February 1, 1952 (predecessor Memphis Recording Service, January 3, 1950) Location: Memphis, Tennessee (706 Union Avenue) Founder: Sam Phillips Key years: 1952–1968 (sold to Shelby Singleton, July 1969) Subsidiaries: Flip Records (1955), Phillips International Records (October 1957) RYM The...

item.title: Abbey Road Studios
item.description: Location: 3 Abbey Road, St John’s Wood, London Years of peak activity: 1931–present (as EMI Studios until 1970, Abbey Road Studios thereafter) Key engineers: Norman Smith, Geoff Emerick, Ken Townsend, Alan Parsons Studio Two at Abbey Road is a wide, high-ceilinged live room, and its warm, natural re...

item.title: Gold Star Studios
item.description: Location: 6252 Santa Monica Boulevard, Hollywood, California Years of peak activity: 1950s–1984 (demolished 1984) Key engineers: Larry Levine, Stan Ross, Dave Gold Gold Star’s echo chambers, a pair of trapezoidal concrete rooms built directly behind Studio A, were the studio’s secret instrument: fee...

item.title: Lott — Love and Theft
item.description: Author: Eric Lott Title: Love and Theft: Blackface Minstrelsy and the American Working Class Year: 1993 Type: Academic book (Oxford University Press) A study of blackface minstrelsy that reframes white America’s relationship with Black culture as simultaneously one of desire and domination.

item.title: Miller — Segregating Sound
item.description: Author: Karl Hagstrom Miller Title: Segregating Sound: Inventing Folk and Pop Music in the Age of Jim Crow Year: 2010 Type: Academic book (Duke University Press) A revisionist history of American music’s racial categories that argues the distinction between “Black music” and “white music” was an ind...

item.title: Toynbee — Making Popular Music
item.description: Author: Jason Toynbee Title: Making Popular Music: Musicians, Creativity and Institutions Year: 2000 Publisher: Arnold (London) / Oxford University Press (New York) Type: Book Toynbee’s target is the Romantic ideal of the solitary genius producing original work from inner inspiration — and his claim...

item.title: Warwick — Girl Groups, Girl Culture
item.description: Author: Jacqueline Warwick Title: Girl Groups, Girl Culture: Popular Music and Identity in the 1960s Year: 2007 Type: Academic book (Routledge Studies in Popular Music) Warwick takes the records the rock canon filed under “disposable teen pop” (the Brill Building product, written by men, run by men)...
